Let me analyze this issue. The request is to implement a JS API function to remove a specific tag from a note in AnkiDroid. Looking at the discussion, it seems like the approach has evolved to implementing two functions:

1. `setNoteTags` (or `replaceNoteTags`) - to set/replace all tags on a note
2. `getTagsFromNote` - to get the current tags from a note

This approach allows users to:
1. Get current tags
2. Modify the array (add/remove as needed)
3. Set the new tags

Let me first look at the existing code to understand the current implementation and then implement the changes.

 75→    /**
 76→     Javascript Interface class for calling Java function from AnkiDroid WebView
 77→     see js-api.js for available functions
 78→     */
 79→
 80→    private val context: Context = activity
 81→
 82→    // Text to speech

 86→    private val speechRecognizer = JavaScriptSTT(context)
 87→
 88→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 89→        apiContract: ApiContract,
 90→        boolean: Boolean,
 91→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Boolean(apiContract.isValid, boolean).toString().toByteArray()
 92→
 93→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 94→        apiContract: ApiContract,
 95→        int: Int,
 96→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Integer(apiContract.isValid, int).toString().toByteArray()
 97→
 98→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 99→        apiContract: ApiContract,
100→        long: Long,
101→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Long(apiContract.isValid, long).toString().toByteArray()
102→
103→    open fun convertToByteArray(
104→        apiContract: ApiContract,
105→        string: String,
106→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.String(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()

108→    /**
109→     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
110→     * @param byteArray
111→     * @return apiContract or null
112→     */
113→    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
114→        try {
115→            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
116→            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
117→            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
118→            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
119→            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
120→            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
121→        } catch (j: JSONException) {
122→            Timber.w(j)
123→            activity.runOnUiThread {
124→                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
125→            }
126→        }
127→        return null
128→    }

130→    /*
131→     * see 02-strings.xml
132→     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
133→     * 1 - mark card
134→     * 2 - flag card
135→     *
136→     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
137→     */
138→    private fun showDeveloperContact(
139→        errorCode: Int,
140→        apiDevContact: String,
141→    ) {
142→        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
143→        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
144→
145→        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
146→            setMaxLines(3)
147→            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
148→                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
149→            }
150→        }
151→    }

153→    /**
154→     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
155→     */
156→    private fun requireApiVersion(
157→        apiVer: String,
158→        apiDevContact: String,
159→    ): Boolean {
160→        try {
161→            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
162→                activity.runOnUiThread {
163→                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
164→                }
165→                return false
166→            }
167→            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.CURRENT_JS_API_VERSION)
168→            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
169→
170→            /*
171→             * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
172→             * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
173→             * otherwise return false
174→             */
175→            return when {
176→                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
177→                    true
178→                }
179→                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
180→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
181→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
182→                    }
183→                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.MINIMUM_JS_API_VERSION))
184→                }
185→                else -> {
186→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
187→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
188→                    }
189→                    false
190→                }
191→            }
192→        } catch (e: Exception) {
193→            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
194→        }
195→        return false
196→    }
197→
198→    /**
199→     * Handle js api request,
200→     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
201→     * @param methodName
202→     * @param bytes
203→     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
204→     * @return
205→     */
206→    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(
207→        methodName: String,
208→        bytes: ByteArray,
209→        returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true,
210→    ) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
211→        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
212→        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
213→        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
214→        // also other action will not be modified
215→        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
216→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
217→        }
218→
219→        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
220→        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
221→
222→        return@withContext when (methodName) {
223→            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
224→            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
225→            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
226→            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
227→            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
228→            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
229→            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
230→            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
231→            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
232→            "toggleFlag" -> {
233→                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
234→                    showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_FLAG_CARD, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
235→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
236→                }
237→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
238→            }
239→            "markCard" ->
240→                processAction({
241→                    activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK)
242→                }, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_MARK_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
243→            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_BURY_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
244→            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_BURT_NOTE, ::convertToByteArray)
245→            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SUSPEND_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
246→            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SUSPEND_NOTE, ::convertToByteArray)
247→            "setCardDue" -> {
248→                try {
249→                    val days = apiParams.toInt()
250→                    if (days < 0 || days > 9999) {
251→                        showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SET_DUE, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
252→                        return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
253→                    }
254→                    activity.launchCatchingTask {
255→                        activity.rescheduleCards(listOf(currentCard.id), days)
256→                    }
257→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
258→                } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
259→                    showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SET_DUE, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
260→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
261→                }
262→            }
263→            "resetProgress" -> {
264→                val cardIds = listOf(currentCard.id)
265→                activity.launchCatchingTask { activity.resetCards(cardIds) }
266→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
267→            }
268→            "cardMark" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.note(getColUnsafe).hasTag(getColUnsafe, "marked"))
269→            "cardFlag" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.userFlag().code)
270→            "cardReps" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.reps)
271→            "cardInterval" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.ivl)
272→            "cardFactor" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.factor)
273→            "cardMod" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.mod)
274→            "cardId" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.id)
275→            "cardNid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.nid)
276→            "cardType" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.type.code)
277→            "cardDid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.did)
278→            "cardLeft" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.left)
279→            "cardODid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDid)
280→            "cardODue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDue)
281→            "cardQueue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.queue.code)
282→            "cardLapses" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.lapses)
283→            "cardDue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.due)
284→            "deckName" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, Decks.basename(activity.getColUnsafe.decks.name(currentCard.did)))
285→            "isActiveNetworkMetered" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, NetworkUtils.isActiveNetworkMetered())
286→            "ttsSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setLanguage(apiParams))
287→            "ttsSpeak" -> {
288→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
289→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
290→                val queueMode = jsonObject.getInt("queueMode")
291→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.speak(text, queueMode))
292→            }
293→            "ttsIsSpeaking" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.isSpeaking)
294→            "ttsSetPitch" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setPitch(apiParams.toFloat()))
295→            "ttsSetSpeechRate" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setSpeechRate(apiParams.toFloat()))
296→            "ttsFieldModifierIsAvailable" -> {
297→                // Know if {{tts}} is supported - issue #10443
298→                // Return false for now
299→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
300→            }
301→            "ttsStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.stop())
302→            "searchCard" -> {
303→                val intent =
304→                    Intent(context, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
305→                        putExtra("currentCard", currentCard.id)
306→                        putExtra("search_query", apiParams)
307→                    }
308→                activity.startActivity(intent)
309→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
310→            }
311→            "searchCardWithCallback" -> ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract)
312→            "isDisplayingAnswer" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isDisplayingAnswer)
313→            "addTagToCard" -> {
314→                activity.runOnUiThread { activity.showTagsDialog() }
315→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
316→            }
317→            "isInFullscreen" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isFullscreen)
318→            "isTopbarShown" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.prefShowTopbar)
319→            "isInNightMode" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isInNightMode)
320→            "enableHorizontalScrollbar" -> {
321→                activity.webView!!.isHorizontalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
322→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
323→            }
324→            "enableVerticalScrollbar" -> {
325→                activity.webView!!.isVerticalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
326→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
327→            }
328→            "showNavigationDrawer" -> {
329→                activity.onNavigationPressed()
330→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
331→            }
332→            "showOptionsMenu" -> {
333→                activity.openOptionsMenu()
334→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
335→            }
336→            "showToast" -> {
337→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
338→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
339→                val shortLength = jsonObject.optBoolean("shortLength", true)
340→                val msgDecode = activity.decodeUrl(text)
341→                showThemedToast(context, msgDecode, shortLength)
342→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
343→            }
344→            "showAnswer" -> {
345→                activity.displayCardAnswer()
346→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
347→            }
348→            "answerEase1" -> {
349→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.AGAIN)
350→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
351→            }
352→            "answerEase2" -> {
353→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.HARD)
354→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
355→            }
356→            "answerEase3" -> {
357→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.GOOD)
358→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
359→            }
360→            "answerEase4" -> {
361→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.EASY)
362→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
363→            }
364→
365→            "addTagToNote" -> {
366→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
367→                val noteId = jsonObject.getLong("noteId")
368→                val tag = jsonObject.getString("tag")
369→                val note =
370→                    getColUnsafe.getNote(noteId).apply {
371→                        addTag(tag)
372→                    }
373→                getColUnsafe.updateNote(note)
374→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
375→            }
376→            "sttSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.setLanguage(apiParams))
377→            "sttStart" -> {
378→                val callback =
379→                    object : JavaScriptSTT.SpeechRecognitionCallback {
380→                        override fun onResult(results: List<String>) {
381→                            activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
382→                                val apiResult = ApiResult.success(Json.encodeToString(ListSerializer(String.serializer()), results))
383→                                val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
384→                                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
385→                            }
386→                        }
387→
388→                        override fun onError(errorMessage: String) {
389→                            activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
390→                                val apiResult = ApiResult.failure(errorMessage)
391→                                val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
392→                                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
393→                            }
394→                        }
395→                    }
396→                speechRecognizer.setRecognitionCallback(callback)
397→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.start())
398→            }
399→            "sttStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.stop())
400→            else -> {
401→                showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_ERROR, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
402→                throw Exception("unhandled request: $methodName")
403→            }
404→        }
405→    }

407→    private fun processAction(
408→        action: () -> Boolean,
409→        apiContract: ApiContract,
410→        errorCode: Int,
411→        conversion: (ApiContract, Boolean) -> ByteArray,
412→    ): ByteArray {
413→        val status = action()
414→        if (!status) {
415→            showDeveloperContact(errorCode, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
416→        }
417→        return conversion(apiContract, status)
418→    }

420→    @NeedsTest("needs coverage")
421→    private suspend fun ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract: ApiContract): ByteArray =
422→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
423→            val cards =
424→                try {
425→                    searchForRows(apiContract.cardSuppliedData, SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering(), CardsOrNotes.CARDS)
426→                        .map { withCol { getCard(it.cardOrNoteId) } }
427→                } catch (exc: Exception) {
428→                    activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript(
429→                        "console.log('${context.getString(R.string.search_card_js_api_no_results)}')",
430→                        null,
431→                    )
432→                    showDeveloperContact(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SEARCH_CARD, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
433→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
434→                }
435→            val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
436→            for (card in cards) {
437→                val jsonObject = JSONObject()
438→                val fieldsData = card.note(getColUnsafe).fields
439→                val fieldsName = card.noteType(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
440→
441→                val noteId = card.nid
442→                val cardId = card.id
443→                jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
444→                jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
445→
446→                val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
447→                fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
448→                    jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
449→                }
450→                jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
451→
452→                searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
453→            }
454→
455→            // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
456→            val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
457→            activity.runOnUiThread {
458→                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
459→            }
460→            convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
461→        }

475→        private val status: JvmBoolean,
476→    ) {
477→        class Boolean(
478→            status: JvmBoolean,
479→            val value: JvmBoolean,
480→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
481→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
482→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
483→            }
484→        }
485→
486→        class Integer(
487→            status: JvmBoolean,
488→            val value: JvmInt,
489→        ) : ApiResult(status) {

491→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)

493→        }

495→        class Float(

501→            }
502→        }
503→
504→        class Long(
505→            status: JvmBoolean,
506→            val value: JvmLong,
507→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
508→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
509→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
510→            }
511→        }
512→
513→        class String(
514→            status: JvmBoolean,
515→            val value: JvmString,
516→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
517→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {

522→        abstract fun putValue(o: JSONObject)

524→        override fun toString() =

531→        @Suppress("RemoveRedundantQualifierName") // we don't want `String(true, value)`
532→        companion object {
533→            fun success(value: JvmString) = ApiResult.String(true, value)

535→            fun failure(value: JvmString) = ApiResult.String(false, value)

539→    class ApiContract(
540→        val isValid: Boolean,
541→        val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact: String,
542→        val cardSuppliedData: String,

545→    companion object {
546→        /**

287→         * Per the manual the next number should be greater than any existing cloze deletion
288→         * even if there are gaps in the sequence, and regardless of existing cloze ordering
289→         *
290→         * @param fieldValues Iterable of field values that may contain existing cloze deletions
291→         * @return the next index that a cloze should be inserted at
292→         */
293→        @KotlinCleanup("general regex fixes for '.group' being nullable")
294→        fun getNextClozeIndex(fieldValues: Iterable<String>): Int {
295→            var highestClozeId = 0
296→            // Begin looping through the fields
297→            for (fieldLiteral in fieldValues) {
298→                // Begin searching in the current field for cloze references
299→                val matcher = clozeRegexPattern.matcher(fieldLiteral)
300→                while (matcher.find()) {
301→                    val detectedClozeId = matcher.group(1)!!.toInt()
302→                    if (detectedClozeId > highestClozeId) {
303→                        highestClozeId = detectedClozeId
304→                    }
305→                }
306→            }
307→            return highestClozeId + 1
308→        }

import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4

import org.json.JSONArray

import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.runner.RunWith

@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
class AnkiDroidJsAPITagsTest : RobolectricTest() {

    @Test
    fun ankiGetNoteTagsTest() =
        runTest {
            val models = col.notetypes
            val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
            val basic = models.byName(BASIC_MODEL_NAME)
            basic!!.put("did", didA)
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")

            // Add some tags to the note
            note.addTag("tag1")
            note.addTag("tag2")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            // Get the note ID
            val noteId = note.id
            val data = JSONObject().apply {
                put("noteId", noteId)
            }.toString()

            val result = jsapi.handleJsApiRequest("getNoteTags", jsApiContract(data), false).decodeToString()
            val jsonResult = JSONObject(result)

            assertThat(jsonResult.getBoolean(SUCCESS_KEY), equalTo(true))

            // The value should be a JSON array string
            val tagsArrayStr = jsonResult.getString(VALUE_KEY)
            val tagsArray = JSONArray(tagsArrayStr)

            // Check that both tags are present
            val tagsList = mutableListOf<String>()
            for (i in 0 until tagsArray.length()) {
                tagsList.add(tagsArray.getString(i))
            }
            assertThat(tagsList.contains("tag1"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(tagsList.contains("tag2"), equalTo(true))
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiSetNoteTagsTest() =
        runTest {
            val models = col.notetypes
            val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
            val basic = models.byName(BASIC_MODEL_NAME)
            basic!!.put("did", didA)
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")

            // Add initial tags
            note.addTag("oldTag")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val noteId = note.id

            // Set new tags
            val data = JSONObject().apply {
                put("noteId", noteId)
                put("tags", JSONArray(listOf("newTag1", "newTag2")))
            }.toString()

            val result = jsapi.handleJsApiRequest("setNoteTags", jsApiContract(data), false).decodeToString()
            assertThat(result, equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))

            // Verify the tags were updated
            val updatedNote = col.getNote(noteId)
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("newTag1"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("newTag2"), equalTo(true))
            // Old tag should be removed
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("oldTag"), equalTo(false))
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiSetNoteTagsEmptyTest() =
        runTest {
            val models = col.notetypes
            val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
            val basic = models.byName(BASIC_MODEL_NAME)
            basic!!.put("did", didA)
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")

            // Add initial tags
            note.addTag("tag1")
            note.addTag("tag2")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val noteId = note.id

            // Set empty tags (remove all)
            val data = JSONObject().apply {
                put("noteId", noteId)
                put("tags", JSONArray())
            }.toString()

            val result = jsapi.handleJsApiRequest("setNoteTags", jsApiContract(data), false).decodeToString()
            assertThat(result, equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))

            // Verify all tags were removed
            val updatedNote = col.getNote(noteId)
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.isEmpty(), equalTo(true))
        }
}

Excellent! All tests pass. Let me summarize what I've implemented:

1. **AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt**: Added two new API handlers:
   - `setNoteTags`: Takes a noteId and an array of tags, replaces all tags on the note with the provided tags
   - `getNoteTags`: Takes a noteId and returns the current tags as a JSON array string

2. **js-api.js**: Added two new JavaScript API methods:
   - `ankiSetNoteTags`: JavaScript method to set note tags (takes noteId and tags array)
   - `ankiGetNoteTags`: JavaScript method to get note tags (takes noteId)

3. Created a new test file **AnkiDroidJsAPITagsTest.kt** with three tests:
   - `ankiGetNoteTagsTest`: Tests getting tags from a note
   - `ankiSetNoteTagsTest`: Tests setting tags on a note (replacing old tags)
   - `ankiSetNoteTagsEmptyTest`: Tests setting empty tags (removing all tags)

The implementation follows the REST-style API design that was discussed in the issue:
- `getNoteTags` returns the current tags as an array
- `setNoteTags` allows setting/replacing the entire tag set
- Users can implement "add tag" and "remove tag" by:
  1. Getting current tags with `getNoteTags`
  2. Modifying the array (adding or removing tags as needed)
  3. Setting the new tags with `setNoteTags`

This approach is more flexible than having separate add/remove methods because it allows multiple tag manipulations in a single operation.

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the implementation:

## Summary of Changes

I've implemented the JS API functions to get and set tags on notes as requested in the issue. The implementation follows the REST-style API design that was discussed:

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt`
- Added import for `com.ichi2.utils.stringIterable` and `org.json.JSONArray`
- Added `setNoteTags` handler that takes a `noteId` and `tags` array, replaces all tags on the note
- Added `getNoteTags` handler that takes a `noteId` and returns the current tags as a JSON array

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/scripts/js-api.js`
- Added `ankiSetNoteTags` to the API list
- Added `ankiGetNoteTags` to the API list
- Added method prototypes for both functions

### 3. New Test File: `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPITagsTest.kt`
- `ankiGetNoteTagsTest`: Verifies getting tags from a note returns correct array
- `ankiSetNoteTagsTest`: Verifies setting tags replaces all existing tags
- `ankiSetNoteTagsEmptyTest`: Verifies setting empty tags removes all tags

## How It Works

With this API, users can now:

1. **Get tags from a note:**
   ```javascript
   const api = new AnkiDroidJS({developer: "test@example.com", version: "0.0.3"});
   const tags = await api.ankiGetNoteTags(noteId);
   // Returns JSON array like: ["tag1", "tag2"]
   ```

2. **Set/replace tags on a note:**
   ```javascript
   await api.ankiSetNoteTags(noteId, ["newTag1", "newTag2"]);
   ```

3. **Remove a specific tag (use case from issue):**
   ```javascript
   const currentTags = await api.ankiGetNoteTags(noteId);
   const tagsArray = JSON.parse(currentTags.value);
   const updatedTags = tagsArray.filter(tag => tag !== "tagToRemove");
   await api.ankiSetNoteTags(noteId, updatedTags);
   ```

48→    val clone = JSONArray()
49→    for (i in 0 until length()) {
50→        clone.put(
51→            when (get(i)) {

282→    object ClozeUtils {
283→        private val clozeRegexPattern = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c(\\d+)::")
284→
285→        /**
286→         * Calculate the next number that should be used if inserting a new cloze deletion.
287→         * Per the manual the next number should be greater than any existing cloze deletion
288→         * even if there are gaps in the sequence, and regardless of existing cloze ordering
289→         *
290→         * @param fieldValues Iterable of field values that may contain existing cloze deletions
291→         * @return the next index that a cloze should be inserted at
292→         */
293→        @KotlinCleanup("general regex fixes for '.group' being nullable")
294→        fun getNextClozeIndex(fieldValues: Iterable<String>): Int {
295→            var highestClozeId = 0
296→            // Begin looping through the fields
297→            for (fieldLiteral in fieldValues) {
298→                // Begin searching in the current field for cloze references
299→                val matcher = clozeRegexPattern.matcher(fieldLiteral)
300→                while (matcher.find()) {
301→                    val detectedClozeId = matcher.group(1)!!.toInt()
302→                    if (detectedClozeId > highestClozeId) {
303→                        highestClozeId = detectedClozeId
304→                    }
305→                }
306→            }
307→            return highestClozeId + 1
308→        }
309→    }
310→}

package com.ichi2.anki

import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPI.Companion.SUCCESS_KEY
import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPI.Companion.VALUE_KEY
import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPITest.Companion.formatApiResult
import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPITest.Companion.getDataFromRequest
import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidJsAPITest.Companion.jsApiContract
import com.ichi2.utils.BASIC_MODEL_NAME
import org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers.equalTo
import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
import org.json.JSONArray
import org.json.JSONObject
import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.runner.RunWith

@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
class AnkiDroidJsAPITagsTest : RobolectricTest() {

    @Test
    fun ankiGetNoteTagsTest() =
        runTest {
            val models = col.notetypes
            val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
            val basic = models.byName(BASIC_MODEL_NAME)
            basic!!.put("did", didA)
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")

            // Add some tags to the note
            note.addTag("tag1")
            note.addTag("tag2")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            // Get the note ID
            val noteId = note.id
            val data = JSONObject().apply {
                put("noteId", noteId)
            }.toString()

            val result = jsapi.handleJsApiRequest("getNoteTags", jsApiContract(data), false).decodeToString()
            val jsonResult = JSONObject(result)

            assertThat(jsonResult.getBoolean(SUCCESS_KEY), equalTo(true))

            // The value should be a JSON array string
            val tagsArrayStr = jsonResult.getString(VALUE_KEY)
            val tagsArray = JSONArray(tagsArrayStr)

            // Check that both tags are present
            val tagsList = mutableListOf<String>()
            for (i in 0 until tagsArray.length()) {
                tagsList.add(tagsArray.getString(i))
            }
            assertThat(tagsList.contains("tag1"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(tagsList.contains("tag2"), equalTo(true))
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiSetNoteTagsTest() =
        runTest {
            val models = col.notetypes
            val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
            val basic = models.byName(BASIC_MODEL_NAME)
            basic!!.put("did", didA)
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")

            // Add initial tags
            note.addTag("oldTag")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val noteId = note.id

            // Set new tags
            val data = JSONObject().apply {
                put("noteId", noteId)
                put("tags", JSONArray(listOf("newTag1", "newTag2")))
            }.toString()

            val result = jsapi.handleJsApiRequest("setNoteTags", jsApiContract(data), false).decodeToString()
            assertThat(result, equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))

            // Verify the tags were updated
            val updatedNote = col.getNote(noteId)
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("newTag1"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("newTag2"), equalTo(true))
            // Old tag should be removed
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("oldTag"), equalTo(false))
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiSetNoteTagsEmptyTest() =
        runTest {
            val models = col.notetypes
            val didA = addDeck("Test", setAsSelected = true)
            val basic = models.byName(BASIC_MODEL_NAME)
            basic!!.put("did", didA)
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")

            // Add initial tags
            note.addTag("tag1")
            note.addTag("tag2")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = ReviewerTest.startReviewer(this)
            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val noteId = note.id

            // Set empty tags (remove all)
            val data = JSONObject().apply {
                put("noteId", noteId)
                put("tags", JSONArray())
            }.toString()

            val result = jsapi.handleJsApiRequest("setNoteTags", jsApiContract(data), false).decodeToString()
            assertThat(result, equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))

            // Verify all tags were removed
            val updatedNote = col.getNote(noteId)
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.isEmpty(), equalTo(true))
        }
}
